evidence: There is reverse coupling in the MediaLightbox module: `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/VideoLightbox.tsx` imports `useVideoLightboxRenderModel`, while `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` imports types from `../VideoLightbox`.

The edit-images tool is tightly bound to MediaLightbox internals: `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts` imports many hooks from `@/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks`, and `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts` imports context/types from MediaLightbox internals.

Supabase access is split across two client entrypoints in the same frontend layer: `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts` use `supabase`, while `src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts` and `src/pages/Home/hooks/auth/useAuthReferralFinalize.ts` use `getSupabaseClient`.

Route-level dependency loading is heavy at startup: `src/app/routes.tsx` eagerly imports multiple major tool pages (`image-generation`, `travel-between-images`, `character-animate`, `join-clips`, `edit-video`) while only a subset is lazy-loaded.

Supabase edge functions repeat URL-pinned dependency imports directly in files (`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts`, `supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts`) instead of a shared import map abstraction.

Critical edge helper exports in supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts (initEdgeRuntime, ensureRequestMethod, edgeErrorResponse) are untested; supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.test.ts only covers parseJsonBody* paths.

supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts has multi-branch behavior (4 orchestrator-ref paths + triggerCostCalculation fetch/error handling) but supabase/functions/_shared/billing.test.ts (27 lines) only asserts 2 extraction shapes and partial filter content.

supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts has separate branches for RPC error return, thrown exception, allowed result, blocked result, and response headers; supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.test.ts covers only thrown-exception fail-open and minimal 429 response assertion.

src/tools/join-clips/lib/clipManager/metadata.ts exports loadClipDuration with error-to-fallback behavior, but existing tests cover getClipsNeedingDuration while loadClipDuration is only mocked by higher-level tests.
`getUserWithTimeout` in `src/shared/lib/toolSettingsService.ts` no longer performs timeout behavior; it only reads cache/localStorage and returns immediately.
Nearby auth helpers in `src/app/routes.tsx` are named by behavior (`hasStoredSession`), highlighting naming drift for similarly scoped auth checks.
naming_quality.issues_preventing_higher_score: A few misleading legacy names remain and make behavior harder to infer at call sites.

evidence: In `waitForVariantDelay` (`src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts`), clearing an existing timeout before creating a new one leaves the earlier Promise unresolved forever.

The current tests (`src/shared/hooks/invalidation/__tests__/useGenerationInvalidation.test.ts`) validate delay timing but do not cover overlapping calls for the same generation key, so this control-flow deadlock is unguarded.
`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts` force-casts query results with `task as unknown as TaskWithProject | null` and then dereferences nested fields, bypassing runtime shape validation.
`supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts` force-casts RPC output (`data as { allowed: boolean; count: number; reset_at: string }`) without guards before constructing logic from those fields.
`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` documents `supabase` export as deprecated compatibility-only and recommends explicit bootstrap via `initializeSupabase()` + `getSupabaseClient()`.
`src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts` still imports and uses the deprecated `supabase` proxy directly, keeping the legacy path active in core query flows.
contract_coherence.issues_preventing_higher_score: Core consumers still rely on deprecated compatibility API, so runtime contract migration is incomplete.

evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exposes a module-level proxy (`export const supabase = createSupabaseCompatibilityProxy()`) that lazily initializes runtime on first property access, making call order matter.
`src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts` accesses this proxy from data hooks, so query execution can implicitly initialize runtime instead of explicit bootstrap.

`src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts` keeps module-level timeout stores (`syncInvalidationTimeouts`, `variantInvalidationTimeouts`) shared across all callers/query clients, coupling independent lifecycles.

findings.summary: MediaLightbox render hook depends back on the component file, creating a brittle bidirectional module dependency.
evidence: `VideoLightbox.tsx` imports `useVideoLightboxRenderModel` from hooks.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` imports `VideoLightboxProps` and related model types from `../VideoLightbox`.
findings.suggestion: Move shared prop/model types into a dedicated neutral module (for example `MediaLightbox/types/videoLightboxModels.ts`) and have both files import from that module to remove the reverse dependency edge.

findings.summary: The edit-images feature is coupled to MediaLightbox internals instead of a stable tool-facing interface.
evidence: `useInlineEditState.ts` imports many internals from `@/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks` and `@/shared/components/MediaLightbox/utils`.
`useImageEditValue.ts` imports internal context and type primitives from MediaLightbox folders.
`InlineEditView.tsx` imports MediaLightbox components and context provider directly (`MediaDisplayWithCanvas`, button groups, `ImageEditProvider`).
findings.suggestion: Introduce an explicit edit-images adapter layer (for example `shared/lib/imageEditRuntime`) that exports only required contracts; migrate edit-images to that API and keep MediaLightbox internals private.

findings.summary: Frontend data/auth hooks use mixed Supabase client entrypoints, increasing dependency surface and initialization variance.
evidence: `useGenerationMutations.ts` and `useTasks.ts` import `supabase` directly from `@/integrations/supabase/client`.
`useDiscordSignIn.ts` and `useAuthReferralFinalize.ts` import and call `getSupabaseClient()` from the same module.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one client acquisition pattern per runtime (prefer a single exported accessor or hook) and provide a thin compatibility wrapper only at boundary modules.

findings.summary: App routing eagerly imports several heavy tool pages, pulling large dependency trees into initial load.
evidence: `routes.tsx` eagerly imports `ImageGenerationToolPage`, `VideoTravelToolPage`, `CharacterAnimatePage`, `JoinClipsPage`, and `EditVideoPage`.
`JoinClipsPage.tsx` itself has broad imports across gallery/lightbox/hooks, compounding route-level eager load weight.
`MediaGallery/index.tsx` is a high-dependency component used in tool pages and contributes to transitive bundle expansion when tool routes are eager.
findings.suggestion: Restore lazy loading for primary tool routes with a guarded hydration-ready boundary (e.g., defer mount until query/runtime bootstrap signal) so Safari workaround does not require full eager imports.

findings.summary: Edge functions duplicate URL-pinned runtime dependencies inline, making upgrades and consistency checks manual.
evidence: `orchestrator.ts` and `generation.ts` each import `SupabaseClient` from `https://esm.sh/@supabase/supabase-js@2.39.7`.
`calculate-task-cost/index.ts` imports from both `https://deno.land/std@0.224.0/http/server.ts` and `https://esm.sh/@supabase/supabase-js@2.39.7` inline.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a centralized Deno import map/module for shared runtime dependencies and reference aliases from all edge functions to enforce single-point version control.

findings.summary: Edge request runtime/method guard APIs are exported and reused but lack direct tests for failure contracts and headers.
evidence: edgeRequest.ts defines initEdgeRuntime (env missing branch), ensureRequestMethod (405 + Allow header + logger.flush), and edgeErrorResponse envelope shaping.
edgeRequest.test.ts only exercises parseJsonBody, parseJsonBodyStrict, and parseJsonBodyStrictResponse; no coverage for initEdgeRuntime/ensureRequestMethod/edgeErrorResponse.
findings.suggestion: Add focused unit tests for (1) missing SUPABASE env vars returning 500 configuration response, (2) method mismatch returning 405 with Allow header and logger flush behavior, and (3) edgeErrorResponse payload/header contract including retryAfter pass-through.

findings.summary: Shared billing helper tests miss critical branches used by multiple edge functions, especially HTTP trigger behavior.
evidence: billing.ts includes four extraction paths in extractOrchestratorRef and a networked triggerCostCalculation path with success/non-OK/exception handling.
billing.test.ts validates only two extraction shapes, one UUID self-check case, and partial filter contains checks; no tests for triggerCostCalculation or full extraction precedence/path matrix.
findings.suggestion: Add table-driven tests covering all documented orchestrator path variants (including invalid/non-object inputs and precedence), plus fetch-mocked tests for triggerCostCalculation success, non-OK text logging, and thrown network errors.

findings.summary: Rate limiting tests do not verify normal RPC success/blocked paths or full response contract headers consumed by callers.
evidence: rateLimit.ts has distinct branches for RPC returned error (fail-open), RPC throw (fail-open), allowed result, blocked result with retryAfter, and header generation (X-RateLimit-* + Retry-After).
rateLimit.test.ts currently asserts preset existence, thrown-error fail-open, and a minimal 429 payload check, but does not assert allowed/blocked RPC result computations or response header contract.
findings.suggestion: Expand tests to cover RPC success paths with deterministic reset/count values, blocked branch retryAfter math, and strict assertions for X-RateLimit-Limit/Remaining/Reset and Retry-After headers plus errorCode payload fields.

findings.summary: Clip metadata loading fallback behavior is untested where it matters most: extraction failure and error-handling side effects.
evidence: metadata.ts loadClipDuration catches extractor failures, calls handleError with structured context, then returns durationSeconds: 0 fallback.
clipManagerService.test.ts includes getClipsNeedingDuration coverage but no loadClipDuration test; useClipManager.test.ts mocks loadClipDuration rather than asserting its behavior.
findings.suggestion: Add unit tests for loadClipDuration success and failure paths with mocked extractVideoMetadataFromUrl and handleError, asserting returned fallback object shape and error context payload.

findings.summary: Supabase compatibility-proxy sunset contract is documented but not backed by an explicit quality gate.
evidence: README.md lists strict compatibility enforcement for `@/types/shots` only.
src/integrations/supabase/client.ts states a removal gate for legacy `supabase` imports in comments, but no automated check/test command is declared alongside that contract.
findings.suggestion: Add a CI-enforced check (wired into `quality:check`) that fails on `import { supabase } from '@/integrations/supabase/client'`, mirroring the shots-shim governance pattern.

findings.summary: Core error-handling facade behavior is contract-heavy but lacks visible governance-level test coverage requirements.
evidence: handleError.ts exposes public contract functions (`handleError`, `handleAndRethrow`, `setErrorPresenter`) with branch behavior (`showToast`, callback invocation, presenter wiring).
README.md quality section does not enumerate any mandatory gate/test suite for this runtime contract surface.
findings.suggestion: Define and enforce a required test suite for error facade contracts (normalization, toast suppression/defaults, callback invocation, and rethrow semantics) and include it in `quality:check`.

findings.summary: Canonical generation/shot type barrel has migration significance but no explicit compile-time contract test requirement.
evidence: generationAndShots.ts is declared as the canonical barrel for generation + shot domain types.
README.md migration policy enforces removal of `@/types/shots` imports, but does not define a type-level API continuity gate for the canonical barrel exports.
findings.suggestion: Add a type-contract test (e.g., tsd or compile-only assertions) that validates required exports from `@/types/generationAndShots` and run it in CI.

findings.summary: Overlapping variant invalidations for the same generation can leave earlier calls awaiting forever.
evidence: `waitForVariantDelay` clears a prior timeout from `variantInvalidationTimeouts` (lines ~100-104) but does not resolve the Promise created by the prior invocation.
The only resolve path is inside the timeout callback (lines ~112-116), so clearing that timeout strands the previous await permanently.
No test covers two concurrent `invalidateVariantChange` calls sharing the same `generationId` with delay.
findings.suggestion: Store and cancel with resolver-aware entries (timeoutId + resolve/reject) so superseded waits complete deterministically, and add a regression test for two overlapping calls on the same generation key.

findings.summary: Deprecated supabase proxy keeps runtime initialization order implicit in data hooks.
evidence: `client.ts` catches runtime-access errors and lazily falls back to initialization inside `getSupabaseClient`, while exporting `supabase` as a compatibility Proxy.
`useTasks.ts` imports `supabase` and issues queries directly, so hook execution can trigger initialization side effects depending on access timing instead of explicit app bootstrap.
findings.suggestion: Migrate hook call sites to `getSupabaseClient()` after guaranteed `initializeSupabase()` at app startup, then remove proxy usage from hot paths.

findings.summary: Critical edge-function flows trust unknown database/RPC payloads through unchecked casts.
evidence: `calculate-task-cost` casts `task` to `TaskWithProject` using `as unknown as` and reads nested properties like `typedTask.projects.user_id` without validating shape.
`rateLimit.ts` casts `data` from RPC directly to `{ allowed; count; reset_at }` and computes retry windows from it without runtime guards.
findings.suggestion: Add small runtime validators/type guards for task row and rate-limit RPC payloads, then branch to explicit error handling when shape checks fail instead of force-casting.

findings.summary: Consumer hooks still depend on API documented as deprecated compatibility-only.
evidence: `client.ts` deprecates `supabase` export with a removal target and recommends `getSupabaseClient()` plus explicit bootstrap.
`useTasks.ts` continues to import `supabase` directly for production queries, contradicting the declared migration contract.
findings.suggestion: Align implementation with documented contract by replacing deprecated proxy imports in hooks and tracking remaining usages until removal criteria are satisfied.

findings.summary: `getUserWithTimeout` name advertises timeout semantics that the implementation no longer provides.
evidence: `getUserWithTimeout` now performs synchronous cache/storage reads and returns resolved Promises; there is no timeout path.
Same area uses behavior-revealing names like `hasStoredSession`, making `getUserWithTimeout` comparatively misleading.
findings.suggestion: Rename to behavior-accurate naming (for example `getCachedOrStoredUser`) and update call sites/docs to avoid implying timeout control.
